companyResolutionResult.description:
Econergy Group (TASE: ECNR) is a leading European Independent Power Producer (IPP) and active developer specializing in solar PV, wind, and energy storage projects across key European markets, including the UK, Italy, Spain, Romania, Poland, and Greece. 
With a robust project pipeline exceeding 8.2 GW in solar PV and wind and an additional 10.6 GWh in energy storage, we are at the forefront of driving Europe’s renewable energy transition.
The company’s local teams in each operational country provide a strategic advantage, ensuring close collaboration with regulatory entities and local communities. 

Our solid strategic partnerships are key to securing project funding and further bolstering its financial stability. Additionally, the company generates revenue across the entire value chain by selling electricity, earning development and operation fees, and selling projects at various stages of development, ensuring a diversified and sustainable income stream.

http://www.econergytech.com
companyResolutionResult.name: Econergy Renewable Energy Ltd.

currentPositions.description:
• Subject matter expert in non-ionizing radiation (RF and ELF), managing risk and ensuring compliance with ICNIRP exposure limits for occupational and public environments.
• Conducted radiation measurements using Narda equipment, calculated safety distances, and developed technical solutions to minimize interference between transmission and receiving systems.
• Advised commanders on radiation risk management, led investigations of safety incidents, and implemented preventive protocols in accordance with IDF regulations (Standing Order 120.002).
• Delivered training programs and lectures for Radiation Safety Officers across subordinate units.
• Managed Electromagnetic Compatibility (EMC): Identified interference sources and provided operational solutions to optimize system performance.

resumePosition.details:
• Developed a RAG chatbot in TypeScript and Node.
• js, delivering intuitive access to platform insights for non-technical users—combining MongoDB vector search and custom NLP detectors to cut search time by 70%.

• Created a Python SDK to streamline automation and manual tasks for users.

resumePosition.details:
• Sensi.
• AI is an audio-based AI startup, raised $54M, 100+ employees, that developed a virtual care and home monitoring platform, allowing early detection of more than 100 health related events of the elderly population.
• I'm leading the expansion of the company into new market segments, mainly payers (health insurers) and home health providers, establishing our GTM strategy and building and proving an ROI model for each of the different segments.

• TytoCare is a Digital health company, raised $205M, 200+ employees, that developed a SaaS platform embedding a kit of connected medical devices enabling a physician to examine patients remotely as part of a video conference or asynchronously using AI-led guidance capabilities featured in a mobile app and an IoT device.
• I led the company’s business, sales and product strategy and GTM execution globally, including penetration into 30 new markets and new segments/verticals in existing markets, driving growth and increasing adoption and usage.
• Forecasting, pipeline management (using Salesforce) and achieving quarterly revenue targets (annual quota of $6-8M ARR), managing a team of BizDev and sales directors, negotiating and closing strategic complex multi-year deals.
• Executive account management with strategic partners and channels to build a joint marketing and product roll-out and initiating new business plans and partnerships.

workExperience.title: Vice President of Global Sales & Business Development

resumePosition.details:
• Launched the product at global markets and establishing the company’s business strategy, pricing models and preferred partners to penetrate new markets.
• Led the business development, account management and sales efforts in those markets, including market analysis, GTM planning, adjustments of pricing model, leads qualification, understanding the regulatory and operational requirements.
• Closing strategic deals with leading health systems, payers and providers, including negotiation of commercial terms, legal, regulatory and operational aspects.
• Managed the global implementation and customer success teams and launching successful large-scale deployments with local partners that showed significant growth.

• Prepared the product towards market launch, bringing the product from a proof-of-concept to a mature ready-to-market phase to support commercialization and roll-out.
• Led the product management activities with R&D, Engineering, Regulation, Operations, Support, Marketing and Sales.
• Leading a team of UX experts and graphic designers and running usability tests.

• Product management of a multi-disciplinary system, which creates highly accurate 3D models using advanced optics and image processing.
• The product is used for quality control of manufacturing industries, especially automotive, aerospace and healthcare.
• End-to-end management of SW & HW versions, including decision of scope and content and defining the requirements according to customer priorities and business potential.
• Building the product roadmap to penetrate new markets and applications, involving adjustment of product capabilities and preparing relevant marketing material.

• Leading the development and commercialization of a software product for 3D image processing and advanced visualization for medical imaging.
• Product Owner for agile R&D teams, which developed a web and mobile applications for real-time collaboration between clinicians.
• Competitors analysis and market research on new trends in the imaging market and building a business plan for a new development project accordingly.

• Overall responsibility of the main product of the company, with emphasis on directing and accompanying the R&D teams (Inbound).
• The product (PACS) enables personalized visualization of medical imaging inside the hospital and remotely, and provides advanced tools for viewing, processing and management of the data to achieve optimized diagnosis.
• Writing detailed product specifications documents and prioritization of requirements against identified customer needs, R&D resources and release plan schedule.

• Project management of a new CRM product, including defining the product specifications, developing SDK tools, coordinating R&D schedule, managing customer requirements, training and involvement in the product architecture.
• Engagement with customers worldwide to adjust product capabilities to their needs.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

companyResolutionResult.description:
The Israel Defense Forces (IDF) is the military of the State of Israel, responsible for the nation's defense and security. Founded in 1948, the IDF ranks among the most battle-tested armed forces in the world, having had to defend the country in six major wars. 

At the age of 18, men and women are required to do mandatory military service in one of the IDF's three branches - Ground Forces, Air Force, and Navy. The Israel Defense Forces is unique in its inclusion of mandatory conscription of women. Men and women of all ranks serve side by side in various positions such as pilots, captains, combat soldiers, intelligence, doctors, technicians and much more. This national service brings together Israelis from all sectors of society, religions and backgrounds. 

The IDF is considered to be one of the world's most technologically-advanced militaries and contributes greatly to Israel's thriving culture of tech start-ups and cutting-edge innovation.
companyResolutionResult.name: Israel Defense Forces

companyResolutionResult.description:
Blockaid is the onchain security platform trusted by chains, protocols, wallets, exchanges, banks, and digital asset managers to understand and secure what matters most. 

Our platform provides unmatched visibility from direct integrations with the most used web3 wallets, plus internet-wide monitoring that detects threats before they reach users.

Not only does this give us the most comprehensive coverage of any security provider, but the more pre-transaction, onchain, offchain, and threat intelligence data our system sees, the more our models improve. 

This flywheel effect has made us the platform of choice for industry leaders like Coinbase, Kraken, MetaMask, Uniswap, World App, and Stellar—as well as traditional institutions and fintechs.
companyResolutionResult.name: Blockaid

companyResolutionResult.description:
As Israel's leading systems integrator, Bynet Data Communications offers comprehensive solutions and services that provide complete answers to organizations’ ICT and telecom needs. Combined with professional services that ensure the success of each and every element of IT infrastructure, Bynet provides a solid foundation for companies’ growth, today and tomorrow.

Bynet provides the full range of solutions for work from the office and company complexes and for remote work using a wide range of technologies such as Multi Cloud, Cyber & Security, DevOps, AI & Big DATA, Data Center and DR,
Collaboration, Contact Center, Networking, Audio, Video, automation and more all in a precise match of the solution to the customer's needs

As part of the STKI 2021 survey, Bynet was ranked in 25 categories as a Tier-1 integrator. In 12 categories, Bynet was ranked first in Israel.

Since Bynet’s inception in 1975, we’ve worked hand in hand with our customers, establishing a solid reputation as a trusted partner in building their comprehensive integration solutions. Relying on our expertise, our clients can be confident that we will build the infrastructure they need. As Bynet is certified to ISO 9001: 2000, ISO 27001 and ISO 13485, they can count on our professionalism and attention to detail

Psagot is the flagship program of the military's academic services. High school graduates with outstanding talents are drafted into critical research and development positions at the core of the IDF's technological activities. Psagot trains engineers, introducing them to wide theoretical backgrounds and providing them with the tools to tackle a multitude of projects.

The program includes intensive studies in challenging fields including electronics, physics, and computer systems. Participants also undergo academic training in either electro-physics or software engineering. Towards the end of their studies, graduates are assigned a variety of roles in defense research and development.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Welcome to the Jerusalem College of Technology (JCT), a leading institution where technological innovation meets traditional Judaic studies in the heart of Jerusalem. At JCT, we provide world-class education in technology, health sciences and business, coupled with a rich Judaic studies program that prepares students for leadership in a globalized world.

About Us:

At JCT, we are committed to excellence in education. Our programs in engineering, computer science, health sciences, business, and more are designed to equip students with cutting-edge technical skills and a strong ethical foundation. Our unique blend of modern technology and Jewish values offers a distinctive educational experience.

Why choose JCT?

Top-Tier Education: Our esteemed faculty deliver high-quality instruction in various fields, preparing our students for their future careers.

Comprehensive Judaic Studies: Students don’t have to choose between academic excellence and spiritual growth. Our Judaic studies program is designed to enhance both. Our top-level shiurim and rich religious environment allow students to deepen their spiritual and ethical development while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Strategic Location: Situated in the historic and vibrant city of Jerusalem, JCT provides a stimulating academic environment enriched by cultural, spiritual and intellectual opportunities.

Supportive Community: We offer a collaborative community where students, faculty, and alumni support each other. Our focus on mentorship and career development ensures that students have the resources they need to excel.

Connect with us to learn more about our programs, events, and opportunities.

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The "IDF School of Computer Science," also known as "Basmach," is a program within the Israeli Defense Forces (IDF) that provides advanced training and education in computer science and related fields to soldiers who have been identified as having exceptional talent in these areas. The program was established in the 1980s as a response to the growing importance of computing and technology in the military.
Soldiers who are accepted into the Basmach program receive intensive training in computer science, software engineering, cybersecurity, and other related fields, as well as practical experience working on real-world military technology projects. The program is highly selective and competitive, and graduates are highly sought after by leading technology companies and startups in Israel and around the world.
The Basmach program is part of a larger trend in the Israeli military of investing in high-tech capabilities and developing the next generation of military technology leaders. Through programs like Basmach, the IDF aims to stay at the forefront of technological innovation and maintain its strategic military advantage in a rapidly changing global security environment.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Basmach - IDF School of Computer Science

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Reichman University (previously IDC Herzliya), the first private institution of higher education in Israel, opened its doors in 1994 thanks to the vision of Prof. Uriel Reichman. Reichman University's ten schools offer innovative, dynamic undergraduate and graduate programs that educate Israel's future business, juridical, high-tech, communications, economics, and civic leaders. 
All of the academic programs provide students with both a solid theoretical background and the practical interdisciplinary skills that are essential for success in the 21st century. 

The university’s alumni are leading key positions at government agencies, high-tech companies, and financial institutions. 

Reichman University is unique in its educational methods, which are based on an interdisciplinary approach and the teaching of information technology and global markets. It combines theory with real-world experience and provides its students with proficiencies. 

Our basic outlook, which is rooted in the twin concepts of individual freedom and responsibility, emphasizes student entrepreneurship and leadership alongside a commitment to community service.

Follow us on all social media channels >>> https://bit.ly/3TVTCJE

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Weizmann Institute of Science is one of the world’s leading multidisciplinary research institutions. 

The Institute has five faculties – Mathematics and Computer Science, Physics, Chemistry, Biochemistry and Biology – and the faculties in turn are divided into 17 scientific departments. Over the years, work by Institute scientists has widely expanded our knowledge in these areas, created new research fields, and combined traditional scientific disciplines in multidisciplinary studies that steer research into new, exciting and often surprising paths.


In addition, the Weizmann School of Science, the Institute’s university arm, trains research students pursuing graduate degrees.


Over the years, the Weizmann Institute has grown with the country, and it has been the site of numerous milestones in Israel’s science. Institute scientists were pioneers in the field of cancer research in Israel. Others planned and built the country’s first electronic computer, one of the first in the world; yet others founded the first nuclear physics department and erected a particle accelerator next door. They were the first to establish a company for transferring knowledge from academia to industry (YEDA Research and Development), and they initiated the founding of a science-based industrial park near the Institute.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Weizmann Institute of Science

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem is Israel's first university, formed in 1918 and opened in 1925. Today, there are over 23,000 students enrolled at the University, including undergraduates, master's degree students, and doctoral candidates.

The university has four campuses: Mount Scopus, The Edmond J. Safra (Givat Ram) Campus, and Ein Kerem (in Jerusalem), and Rehovot.

Hebrew University Alumni are invited to visit https://alumni.huji.ac.il

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Welcome to the Jerusalem College of Technology (JCT), a leading institution where technological innovation meets traditional Judaic studies in the heart of Jerusalem. At JCT, we provide world-class education in technology, health sciences and business, coupled with a rich Judaic studies program that prepares students for leadership in a globalized world.

About Us:

At JCT, we are committed to excellence in education. Our programs in engineering, computer science, health sciences, business, and more are designed to equip students with cutting-edge technical skills and a strong ethical foundation. Our unique blend of modern technology and Jewish values offers a distinctive educational experience.

Why choose JCT?

Top-Tier Education: Our esteemed faculty deliver high-quality instruction in various fields, preparing our students for their future careers.

Comprehensive Judaic Studies: Students don’t have to choose between academic excellence and spiritual growth. Our Judaic studies program is designed to enhance both. Our top-level shiurim and rich religious environment allow students to deepen their spiritual and ethical development while pursuing their academic and professional goals.

Strategic Location: Situated in the historic and vibrant city of Jerusalem, JCT provides a stimulating academic environment enriched by cultural, spiritual and intellectual opportunities.

Supportive Community: We offer a collaborative community where students, faculty, and alumni support each other. Our focus on mentorship and career development ensures that students have the resources they need to excel.

Connect with us to learn more about our programs, events, and opportunities.

Stay Connected: Follow our LinkedIn page for updates, news, and insights. Visit our website or contact us for more information.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Ben-Gurion University of the Negev is one of Israel’s leading research universities and among the world leaders in many fields. 

It has around 20,000 students and 4,000 faculty members in the Faculties of Engineering Sciences; Health Sciences; Natural Sciences; the Pinchas Sapir Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences; the Guilford Glazer Faculty of Business and Management; the Joyce and Irving Goldman School of Medicine; the Kreitman School of Advanced Graduate Studies; and the Albert Katz International School for Desert Studies. More than 100,000 alumni play important roles in all areas of research and development, industry, health care, the economy, society, culture and education in Israel.

The University has three main campuses: The Marcus Family Campus in Beer-Sheva; the research campus at Sede Boqer and the Eilat Campus, and is home to national and multi-disciplinary research institutes: the National Institute for Biotechnology in the Negev; the National Institute of Solar Energy; the Ilse Katz Institute for Nanoscale Science and Technology; the Jacob Blaustein Institutes for Desert Research; the Ben-Gurion Research Institute for the Study of Israel & Zionism, and Heksherim - The Research Institute for Jewish and Israeli Literature and Culture.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Ben-Gurion University of the Negev

organizationUrnResolutionResult.headquartersLocation: Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel
organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ORT colleges are part of ORT Israel, which was established in 1949 and now includes 150 educational institutions. The network trains students to cope with the accelerated rate of development in the economy and in industry.

ORT colleges devise study curricula adapted to the needs of the country and to future needs, in collaboration with other bodies, such as: the Ministry of Science and Technology, the Ministry of Industry, Trade & Employment, the IDF, high-tech industries and other leading industries in the national economy, both in the private and public sectors.

Our graduates are integrated in the employment market in a wide range of key positions, in high-tech industries, other leading industries, government ministries, in the private market and in a large number of places of employment that require high quality professionals in the technological fields, at all levels.

ORT colleges operate as interdisciplinary education centers, ranging from science and technology studies to the arts, design and communications. The colleges aim to provide a solution for every student, enabling them to successfully meet local and global technological requirements.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: ORT Colleges

companyResolutionResult.description:
Elmo Motion Control is a global leading company of motion control intelligence solution.
Since 1988 the Company designs and manufactures ultra-advanced servo drives and network motion controllers that are small, smart and simple cutting-edge solutions for any need. Focusing on performance, reliability, safety and standards compliance, our products are integrated in a wide variety of applications from industrial to extreme environment automation. 

About Elmo
- 250 employees including 70 engineering experts focused exclusively on R&D 
- Worldwide sales and support network: North America, Europe, and APAC 



For more information, visit www.elmomc.com
companyResolutionResult.name: Elmo Motion Control

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
The Hebrew University of Jerusalem is Israel's first university, formed in 1918 and opened in 1925. Today, there are over 23,000 students enrolled at the University, including undergraduates, master's degree students, and doctoral candidates.

The university has four campuses: Mount Scopus, The Edmond J. Safra (Givat Ram) Campus, and Ein Kerem (in Jerusalem), and Rehovot.

Hebrew University Alumni are invited to visit https://alumni.huji.ac.il
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: The Hebrew University of Jerusalem

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
ביה"ס הריאלי העברי בחיפה הנו מוסד חינוכי עצמאי (וחל"ץ). תלמידינו, המתקבלים לביה"ס בתהליך של מיון ובחירה, נהנים ממסלול חינוך רצוף מגן ועד י"ב, ומתחנכים לאור חזון ביה"ס: "להיות מוסד חינוכי איכותי, מוביל ומתווה דרך, המכוון את תלמידיו להיות בני אדם, מעורבים ומשפיעים בחברה הישראלית".

מאז היווסדו בשנת תרע"ד (1913) על רקע "מלחמת השפות" והדרישה לייסד בית ספר ריאלי שיהיה כולו עברי – התפתח ביה"ס לאורם של מאורעות מרכזיים ביישוב היהודי ובמדינה תוך שהוא משמש חלוץ ומצפן בעשייה החינוכית בישראל. בין המפעלים החינוכיים ש"נולדו" אצלנו ואומצו על ידי משרד החינוך ניתן למנות את ייסוד הצופים, הגדנ"ע, "השירות הלאומי", ה"מחויבות האישית", ה"חיבור השנתי", "חברת התלמידים", מקצועות האזרחות, המזרחנות ועוד.

כיום מונה ביה"ס כ- 4,000 תלמידים בשישה סניפים וכ- 500 אנשי סגל, כ- 80 מתוכם בוגרי ביה"ס. לכל סניף בביה"ס מאפיין ייחודי משלו, תחת תפיסה חינוכית כוללת:

מתחם הדר - מדעים ואומנויות
מרכז הכרמל (מטו"ס) - מדע, טבע וסביבה
מתחם אחוזה - מנהיגות ותקשורת
החטיבה העליונה בבית בירם המציעה מעל 50 צירופי מקצועות
אשכול גנים ירוק - בקמפוס בית בירם

התלמידים שלנו מגיעים לביה"ס מרחבי העיר חיפה ומהפריפריה, מנהריה בצפון דרך יקנעם במזרח ועד זיכרון יעקב בדרו�...

organizationUrnResolutionResult.description:
Approximately 36 years before Israel declared independence, a ceremony took place on the barren slopes of Mount Carmel near the Port of Haifa, which was then occupied by the Ottoman Empire. Unknown to the Haifa community witnessing the event, this would be a milestone in history. This first cornerstone embodied an implausible vision of creating a world-class institute of scientific and technological education in the Holy Land.
The story of the “Technikum” – the original German name of the Technion – is a tale of the century. The second industrial revolution created the printing presses and communications infrastructure allowing Jews scattered around the globe to organize in face of rising anti-Semitism. As Jews were often barred from technical education, the establishment of a technical school was a first priority to rebuilding a Jewish homeland. The Technion was to become a unique worldwide university that would precede, create, shape, and protect a modern state.
The cornerstone laid on April 11, 1912, set in motion a century of progress responding to national and global needs. Technion – Israel Institute of Technology would grow rapidly, becoming a global pioneer in fields such as biotechnology, stem cell research, space, computer science, nanotechnology, and energy. 
Four Technion professors have won Nobel Prizes. As it approaches its centennial in 2025 – 100 years after the first class took place – Technion City is a thriving world center of trailblazing excellence in research and teaching for the benefit of humanity, with 18 faculties, more than 14,000 students, 135 academic programs, and 60 research institutes. Since the Technion opened its doors, more than 90,000 Technion alumni have played a huge part in creating and leading the ‘Startup Nation,’ impacting the lives of millions of people worldwide.
organizationUrnResolutionResult.name: Technion - Israel Institute of Technology

companyResolutionResult.description: At Johnson & Johnson MedTech, we are working to solve the world’s most pressing healthcare challenges through innovations at the intersection of biology and technology. We are developing the next generation of med tech solutions to tackle the most pervasive and complex health challenges for people around the world.

companyResolutionResult.description:
At Brainlab, we digitize medical workflows, from diagnosis to therapy, to offer clinicians and patients better treatment possibilities. Our innovative digital ecosystem forms the basis for modern healthcare technology in 6,700 hospitals in 127 countries. At the forefront of health technology for over 35 years, Munich-based Brainlab employs around 2,400 people with expertise across the entire healthcare value chain in 25 locations worldwide. 

At Brainlab, we digitize medical workflows from diagnosis to therapy, and our innovative ecosystem forms the basis for modern healthcare technology. We help healthcare providers build three-dimensional patient-specific models with data that is structured, mapped and aggregated with industry-leading artificial intelligence. This builds the basis of our navigation system for the human body, clinicians can safely remove brain tumors, place screws in the spine precisely, and irradiate breast tumors with millimetric precision.

Our robotics and mixed reality technologies and expertise allow users to connect and innovate between the digital and physical worlds. In addition, Brainlab technologies power the collection of structured long-term data and medical registries. Through the neuroscience of play, Brainlab advances clinical training and education, promoting the adaptation to technological advances and creating digital models of complete interventions. Our digital ecosystem is an open interface and forms a framework for third parties to accelerate innovation in other clinical areas as well.

https://www.brainlab.com/legal-notice/
https://www.brainlab.com/privacy-policy/
companyResolutionResult.name: Brainlab
